meta.title: National Fire Alarm and Signaling Code
meta.scope: Comprehensive fire alarm system design, installation, testing, and maintenance
meta.jurisdiction: Primary standard in USA, widely adopted in MENA, adopted by reference in many countries

core_principles.purpose: The primary purpose of a fire alarm system is to provide early warning of fire to allow building occupants to safely evacuate and fire departments to respond.
fundamental_requirements: Detection of fire or its products of combustion
Notification of the fire department or emergency services
Control of building systems (elevators, HVAC, doors, etc.)
Annunciation of fire location for emergency response

conventional.description: Devices wired to zones; identifies zone of alarm, not individual device
cons: Cannot identify specific device in alarm
conventional.typical_use: Small buildings, simple layouts, budget projects

addressable.description: Each device has a unique address; panel communicates individually with every device
addressable.typical_use: Medium to large buildings, hospitals, high-rise, industrial

analog_intelligent.description: Addressable devices that send actual sensor readings (smoke density, temperature) to the panel
analog_intelligent.typical_use: Critical facilities, data centers, museums, pharmaceutical

wireless.description: Devices communicate via radio frequency; no wiring between devices and panel
wireless.typical_use: Retrofit projects, historic buildings, temporary installations

hybrid.description: Combination of wired and wireless technology
hybrid.typical_use: Building expansions, mixed occupancy, phased projects

automatic.description: System activated by fire detection devices (smoke, heat, flame, gas detectors)

manual.description: System activated by human operation (pull stations, call points)
required_in: Must be within 5 ft (1.5m) of exit doors
manual.notes: Manual systems alone are rarely sufficient; usually combined with automatic detection

automatic_with_manual.description: Combined automatic detection and manual initiation — standard requirement
automatic_with_manual.notes: This is the most common and code-required configuration

single_station.description: Self-contained alarm units (battery-operated smoke alarms in residential)
single_station.notes: Not a building-wide fire alarm system; standalone devices

multiple_station.description: Interconnected single-station alarms that activate all units when one triggers
multiple_station.notes: Provides whole-building notification in residential settings

public_mode.description: Alerts ALL building occupants — total evacuation signal
public_mode.signal_type: Temporal Code 3 (T-3): 0.5s ON, 0.5s OFF, 0.5s ON, 0.5s OFF, 0.5s ON, 1.5s OFF, repeat

private_mode.description: Alerts only designated personnel (staff, security, management)
private_mode.notes: Must be able to convert to public mode if evacuation is needed

voice_evacuation.description: Uses speakers with live or pre-recorded voice messages for evacuation instructions

requirements.intelligibility: Minimum 0.50 Common Intelligibility Scale (CIS) or 0.70 Speech Transmission Index (STI)
requirements.speakers: Distributed for even coverage, minimum 15 dBA above ambient
requirements.zones: Must allow selective paging by zone/floor

emergency_voice_alarm_communication_syst.description: Full EVACS with live paging capability, zone selection, and firefighter phone

pull_station_placement.rule: Pull stations shall be located within 5 ft (1.5m) of the entrance to each exit
pull_station_placement.height: 3.5 ft to 4.5 ft (1.07m to 1.37m) from floor to operable part
exceptions: Where approved by AHJ for specific building conditions

travel_distance_to_pull_station.rule: Maximum travel distance to the nearest manual station shall not exceed 200 ft (61m)
travel_distance_to_pull_station.notes: Measured along the normal path of egress

smoke_detector.description: Point spacing for spot-type smoke detectors on smooth ceilings
smoke_detector.measurement: Measured from detector center to detector center
smoke_detector.wall_distance: Maximum distance from detector to a wall = half the nominal spacing = 15 ft (4.6m)
smoke_detector.notes: This is the NOMINAL spacing. Actual spacing depends on ceiling height, airflow, and obstructions.

rate_of Rise.nominal_spacing: 50 ft (15.2m) — but typically installed at 15-30 ft to match smoke detector spacing

71C_160F.notes: Used in environments with higher ambient temperatures

100C_212F.notes: Boiler rooms, kitchens, industrial process areas

rules.treatment: Reduce spacing — detectors within 3 ft (0.9m) of peak
Detectors within 3 ft (0.9m) of peak AND additional row at bottom of slope

beamed_ceilings.description: Beam depth affects detector placement requirements

rules.treatment: Install detectors in each pocket (space between beams)
Install detectors on the bottom of beams AND in each pocket

ceiling_height_corrections.description: Spacing must be reduced for high ceilings due to smoke stratification

corrections.notes: Smoke detectors may need to be multi-criteria or aspirating for reliability

corrections.smoke_spacing_modifier: Consider aspirating smoke detection (ASD) or beam detectors
corrections.notes: NFPA 72 does not provide standard spacing above 30 ft for spot-type smoke detectors

elevator_shafts.additional: Some jurisdictions require pit detector as well

elevator_lobbies.location: In front of each elevator door on every floor
elevator_lobbies.purpose: Phase 1 recall — sends elevator to designated level

HVAC_ducts.duct_detector_required: When HVAC system >2000 CFM and serving more than one floor, or as required by mechanical code
HVAC_ducts.location: In supply return air duct upstream of any filter, cooling coil, or air treatment device
HVAC_ducts.response: Shuts down HVAC fan to prevent smoke spread

stairwells.detectors: Smoke detectors at each floor level in enclosed stairwells

corridors.spacing: 30 ft (9.1m) on center for smoke detectors
corridors.coverage_per_detector: 30 ft x 15 ft (9.1m x 4.6m) — corridor mode
corridors.notes: Corridor is defined as width ≤ 20 ft (6.1m)

storage_areas.recommendation: Rate-of-rise heat detectors (faster response to rapid temperature increase)
storage_areas.ceiling_height_note: High-bay storage may require beam detectors or ASD

kitchens.recommendation: Heat detectors only — avoid smoke detectors due to nuisance alarms

bathrooms.recommendation: Avoid smoke detectors — steam causes false alarms

parking_garages.recommendation: CO detectors or beam smoke detectors
parking_garages.notes: Standard spot smoke detectors unreliable due to air movement and exhaust

general_requirements.coverage: All occupiable spaces must receive notification
general_requirements.audible_minimum: 15 dBA above ambient OR 5 dBA above maximum ambient, whichever is greater
general_requirements.audible_absolute_minimum: 75 dBA OR 15 dBA above ambient, whichever is greater
general_requirements.visible_minimum: Required in all areas where audible minimum cannot be met, plus all public areas
general_requirements.mounting_height_wall: 80 in to 96 in (2.03m to 2.44m) from floor

temporal_code_3.pattern: 0.5s ON → 0.5s OFF → 0.5s ON → 0.5s OFF → 0.5s ON → 1.5s OFF → REPEAT
temporal_code_3.purpose: Universal evacuation signal — must be used for public mode notification
temporal_code_3.notes: NOT to be confused with temporal code 4 (CO alarms) or other patterns

intensity.rooms: 0.0375 × room area (sq ft) = minimum cd, OR 1 cd per 20 sq ft
intensity.maximum_per_room: 110 cd for wall-mounted, 177 cd for ceiling-mounted

spacing.wall_mount: 100 cd strobe: covers up to 50 ft (15.2m) from device; 110 cd: 55 ft (16.8m)

strobe_requirements.flash_rate: 1 to 2 Hz (60 to 120 flashes per minute)
strobe_requirements.color: White or amber (amber permitted only if building owner requests)

horn_requirements.frequency: 3100 Hz (Horn), 2400 Hz + 670 Hz (Temporal) — varies by manufacturer

speaker_requirements.coverage_radius: Approximately 15 ft (4.6m) per speaker at appropriate tap setting
speaker_requirements.intelligibility: CIS ≥ 0.50 or STI ≥ 0.70 — must be measured in field
speaker_requirements.ambient_noise: Speaker output must be 15 dBA above ambient or 5 dBA above average, whichever is greater, minimum 75 dBA

requirements: Located at point of entry used by fire department
In a room dedicated to fire alarm / building systems
Wall-mounted: center 48 in (1.22m) from floor (recommended 60 in / 1.52m)

annunciator.location: Main entrance, fire command center, or security desk
requirements: Shows all zones with LED indicators (Normal / Trouble / Alarm / Supervisory)
Labels must be permanent and match floor plans

power_supplies.primary: Dedicated 120VAC or 240VAC circuit from building power
power_supplies.battery_calculation: Must account for all connected loads during alarm condition

supervision.description: Panel must monitor integrity of all wiring and connections
supervision.trouble_signal: Distinct audible and visual signal when any monitored circuit faults

class_A.description: Redundant path — system continues to operate if a single wire break occurs
class_A.requirement: Return conductor required; end-of-line device not needed
class_A.typical_use: Life safety critical paths, high-rise, healthcare

class_B.description: Single path — circuit goes into trouble if wire breaks

class_C.notes: Provides equivalent functionality to Class A through digital means

class_E.notes: Must meet signal strength and supervision requirements

wire_types.FPL: Fire Protective Signaling Cable — general purpose
wire_types.notes: Must use appropriate rating for the space (plenum, riser, general)

12_AWG.typical_use: Very long runs, high device count

conductor_sizing.voltage_drop_limit: Maximum 10% voltage drop from panel to farthest device

elevator_recall.trigger: Smoke detector in elevator lobby OR machine room
elevator_recall.action_phase1: All elevators recalled to designated recall floor (usually ground)
elevator_recall.action_phase2: If smoke detected in elevator shaft — all elevators recalled to designated alternate floor
elevator_recall.inputs_to_panel: Elevator lobby smoke detectors, hoistway smoke detectors, machine room heat detectors
elevator_recall.notes: Firefighter's service must be operable during recall

hvac_shutdown.trigger: Duct smoke detector OR zone smoke detector (depending on design)
hvac_shutdown.action: Shut down affected HVAC zone air handlers
hvac_shutdown.purpose: Prevent smoke migration through HVAC system
hvac_shutdown.notes: May also close fire/smoke dampers via separate fire alarm interface

door_release.trigger: Smoke detector in area OR general alarm signal
door_release.action: Release electromagnetic holders on fire doors and smoke barriers
door_release.purpose: Close fire-rated doors to compartmentalize building
door_release.notes: Must fail-safe (doors close on power loss)

sprinkler_system.water_flow_switch: Detects water flowing through sprinkler pipe — indicates activated sprinkler
sprinkler_system.tamper_switch: Detects when sprinkler control valve is closed (system impaired)
sprinkler_system.signal: Water flow = alarm signal; Valve tamper = supervisory signal

fire_pump.monitor: Running status, power failure, phase reversal, low oil pressure (diesel)
fire_pump.notes: May require separate fire alarm monitoring points

stairway_pressurization.trigger: Smoke detection in stairwell or adjacent area
stairway_pressurization.purpose: Keep stairwells clear of smoke for evacuation

access_control.action: Unlock electronic access control doors to allow free egress
access_control.notes: Must fail-safe (doors unlock on power loss)

kitchen_hood_suppression.monitor: System activation (alarm signal), system off (supervisory), system trouble
kitchen_hood_suppression.notes: Usually includes gas shut-off valve monitoring

smoke_control.description: Complex smoke management systems in atriums, malls, and high-rise buildings
smoke_control.notes: Requires dedicated smoke control panel with sequence of operations

acceptance_testing.description: Required upon initial installation or modification
includes: Functional test of each initiating device
Functional test of each notification appliance
Verification of zone mapping and annunciator
Voltage drop verification on all circuits

periodic_inspection.annual: 100% testing of all devices, battery load test, full system inspection
periodic_inspection.semi_annual: Test 50% of devices (alternating halves each 6 months)
periodic_inspection.monthly: Visual inspection, verify panel is normal
periodic_inspection.weekly: Verify no trouble signals on panel
periodic_inspection.notes: Local AHJ may require more frequent testing

shop_drawings_must_include: Floor plans showing device locations (symbols per NFPA 170)
Zone mapping and device listing per zone
Voltage drop calculations for all circuits
Battery calculation with standby and alarm loads
Sequence of operations for all integration interfaces
Manufacturer data sheets for all equipment
documentation_requirements.as_built_drawings: Required after installation — must reflect actual field conditions
documentation_requirements.record_documents: Must be provided to building owner within 30 days of acceptance
